questions.explanation:
The set of all first elements of the ordered pairs in R is called domain of relation.
i.e. domain (R) : {a : (a, b) {tex} \in{/tex} R}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Domain (R) = {1, 3, 4}

questions.question: Domain and range of f(x) = $\frac{|x-3|}{x-3}$ are respectively.
questions.question_raw: Domain and range of f(x) = {tex}\frac{|x-3|}{x-3}{/tex} are respectively.
questions.explanation:
The given function is f(x) = {tex}\frac{|x-3|}{x-3}{/tex}
This function is well defined for all real numbers other than 3.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Its domain is R - {3}
Now, f(x) = {tex}\frac{|x-3|}{x-3}{/tex}
{tex}=\left\{\begin{array}{l} \frac{x-3}{x-3}: x>3 \\ \frac{-(x-3)}{x-3}: x<3 \end{array}=\left\{\begin{array}{l} 1: x>3 \\ -1: x<3 \end{array}\right.\right.{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Range of function f is {1, -1}

questions.question: Let the relation R be defined in N by aRb, if 2a + 3b = 30. Then, R = ________.
questions.question_raw: Let the relation R be defined in N by aRb, if 2a + 3b = 30. Then, R = ________.
questions.explanation:
Given that, 2a + 3b = 30
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3b = 30 - 2a
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}b=\frac{30 \ - \ 2 a}{3}{/tex}
For a = 3, b = 8
a = 6, b = 6
a = 9, b = 4
a = 12, b = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} R = {(3, 8) (6, 6), (9, 4), (12, 2)}

questions.question: Let R = {[a, a^3) : a is a prime number less than 5} be a relation. Find the range of R.
questions.question_raw: Let R = {[a, a3) : a is a prime number less than 5} be a relation. Find the range of R.
questions.explanation:
Given, R = {[a, a3) : a is a prime number less than 5}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = {(2, 8), (3, 27)}
Hence, range of R = {8, 27}.

questions.question: If A = {x $\in$ Z : 0 $\leq$ x $\leq$ 12} and R is the relation in A given by R = {(a, b) : |a - b| is a multiple of 4}. Then, the set of all elements related to 1 is
questions.question_raw: If A = {x {tex}\in{/tex} Z : 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} x {tex}\leq{/tex} 12} and R is the relation in A given by R = {(a, b) : |a - b| is a multiple of 4}. Then, the set of all elements related to 1 is
questions.explanation:
The set of elements related to 1 is {1, 5, 9}.
Since, |1 - 1| = 0 is multiple of 4
|5 - 1| = 4 is a multiple of 4
and |9 - 1| = 8 is a multiple of 4

{tex}\because{/tex} f : R {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R defined by
{tex} f(x)=\sqrt{x^2-3 x+2} {/tex}
Here, x2 - 3x + 2 {tex}\geq {/tex} 0
(x - 1) (x - 2) {tex}\geq {/tex} 0
x {tex}\leq{/tex} 1 or x {tex}\geq {/tex} 2

questions.question: Let A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {2, 4, 6, 8}. Consider the rule f : A $\rightarrow$ B, defined f(x) = 2x, $\forall x \in$ A, then range of f is given by set
questions.question_raw: Let A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {2, 4, 6, 8}. Consider the rule f : A {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} B, defined f(x) = 2x, {tex}\forall x \in{/tex} A, then range of f is given by set

Value of function at x = 1, f(1) = 2(1) = 2
Value of function at x = 2, f(2) = 2(2) = 4

We Can write it as f = {(1, 2), (2, 4), (3, 6)}

questions.question: If f(x) = |cos x|, then $\text f\left(\frac{3 \pi}{4}\right)$ is
questions.question_raw: If f(x) = |cos x|, then {tex}\text f\left(\frac{3 \pi}{4}\right){/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}

questions.question:
Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be a function defined by
$ f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cl} x^2 \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{x^2}\right), & \text { if } x \neq 0 \\ 0, & \text { if } x \neq 0 \end{array}\right. $
Then which of the following statements is TRUE?
questions.question_raw:
Let {tex}f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}{/tex} be a function defined by
{tex} f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cl} x^2 \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{x^2}\right), & \text { if } x \neq 0 \\ 0, & \text { if } x \neq 0 \end{array}\right. {/tex}
Then which of the following statements is TRUE?

options.text: $f(x)=0$ has infinitely many solutions in the interval $\left[\frac{1}{10^{10}}, \infty\right)$.
$f(x)=0$ has no solutions in the interval $\left[\frac{1}{\pi}, \infty\right)$.
$f(x)=0$ has more than 25 solutions in the interval $\left(\frac{1}{\pi^2}, \frac{1}{\pi}\right)$.
The set of solutions of $f(x)=0$ in the interval $\left(0, \frac{1}{10^{10}}\right)$ is finite.

questions.explanation:
1. {tex}f(x)=x^2 \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{x^2}\right){/tex}
    {tex} f(x)=0 \Rightarrow x^2 \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{x^2}\right)=0 (\text { As } x \neq 0) {/tex}
    {tex} \Rightarrow \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{x^2}\right)=0 {/tex}
    We have, {tex}\frac{\pi}{x^2}=n \pi, n \in Z ; x^2=\frac{1}{n} \Rightarrow x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}{/tex}
    {tex} \therefore \frac{1}{10^{10}} \leq \frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}<\infty \text { i.e. } 10^{10} \geq \sqrt{n}>0{/tex}
    {tex}\sqrt{n} \leq 10^{10} \Rightarrow n \leq 10^{20}{/tex}. So, it has finite number of solutions.
  2. We require {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \geq \frac{1}{\pi} \Rightarrow \sqrt{n} \leq \pi \Rightarrow n \leq \pi^2{/tex}
    It has at least one solution.
  3. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}<\frac{1}{10^{10}} \Rightarrow \sqrt{n}>10^{10}{/tex} is {tex}n>10^{20}{/tex}
    Thus, the number of solutions is infinite.
  4. {tex}\frac{1}{\pi^2}<\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}<\frac{1}{\pi} \Rightarrow \pi^2>\sqrt{n}>\pi{/tex} i.e. {tex}\pi^2<n<\pi^4{/tex}
    It has more than 25 solutions.

questions.question: The domain of the function $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1}\right)}{\pi}\right)$ is:
questions.question_raw: The domain of the function {tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1}\right)}{\pi}\right){/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1}\right)}{\pi}\right){/tex}
As we know the domain of {tex}\cos ^{-1} y{/tex} is {tex}[-1,1]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow -1 \leq\left(\frac{2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1}\right)}{\pi}\right) \leq 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow -\frac{\pi}{2} \leq \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1}\right) \leq \frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow -1 \leq \frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1} \leq 1{/tex}
{tex}\text { So, } \frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1} \geq-1 \text { and } \frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1} \leq 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1}+1 \geq 0 \text { and } \frac{1}{4 x^{2}-1}-1 \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4 x^{2}}{4 x^{2}-1} \geq 0 \text { and } \frac{2-4 x^{2}}{4 x^{2}-1} \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}}{(2 x-1)(2 x+1)} \geq 0{/tex}
{tex}\text { and } \frac{(1-\sqrt{2} x)(1+\sqrt{2} x)}{(2 x-1)(2 x+1)} \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \in\left(-\infty,-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) \cup\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}, \infty\right) \cup\{0\}{/tex}
{tex}\text { and } x \in\left(-\infty, \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) \cup\left(-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{1}{2}\right) \cup\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}, \infty\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \in\left(-\infty,-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right) \cup\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}, \infty\right) \cup\{0\}{/tex}

questions.question: The number of elements in the set $\{x \in {R}$ : $(|x|-3)|x+4|=6\}$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: The number of elements in the set {tex}\{x \in {R}{/tex} : {tex}(|x|-3)|x+4|=6\}{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given,

Case I: when {tex}x \lt -4{/tex}
Since, {tex}|x|=\left\{\begin{array}{l}x ; x \gt 0 \\ -x ; x \lt 0\end{array}\right.{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow (-x-3)(-x-4)=6{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow (x+3)(x+4)=6{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{2}+7 x+6=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow (x+6)(x+1)=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=-1{/tex} and -6
As {tex}x \lt -4{/tex} so for this case {tex}x=\{-6\}{/tex}
Case II: when {tex}x \in[-4,0){/tex}
Using {tex}|x|=\left\{\begin{array}{l}x ; x \geq 0 \\ -x ; x \lt 0\end{array}\right.{/tex}
{tex}(-x-3)(x+4)=6{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{2}+7 x+18=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} No solution ({tex}{D} \lt 0{/tex})
So, for this case {tex}x \in \phi{/tex}
Case III:
When {tex}x \geq 0{/tex}
{tex}(x-3)(x+4)=6{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{2}+x-18=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{-1 \pm \sqrt{1+72}}{2}{/tex}
As {tex}x \geq 0{/tex} so for this case {tex}x=\left\{\frac{\sqrt{73}-1}{2}\right\}{/tex}
So, final solution of the given equation are
{tex}x \in\left\{-6, \frac{\sqrt{73}-1}{2}\right\}{/tex}
Hence number of solution will be 2.

questions.question: The range of $a \in {R}$ for which the function $f(x)=(4 a-3)\left(x+\log _{{e}} 5\right)$ $+2(a-7) \cot \left(\frac{x}{2}\right) \sin ^{2}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right),$ $ x \neq 2 n \pi, n \in {~N}$ has critical points, is:
questions.question_raw: The range of {tex}a \in {R}{/tex} for which the function {tex}f(x)=(4 a-3)\left(x+\log _{{e}} 5\right){/tex} {tex}+2(a-7) \cot \left(\frac{x}{2}\right) \sin ^{2}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right),{/tex} {tex} x \neq 2 n \pi, n \in {~N}{/tex} has critical points, is:
questions.explanation:
Given that
{tex}f(x)=(4 a-3)\left(x+\log _{e} 5\right){/tex}{tex}+2(a-7) \cot \left(\frac{x}{2}\right) \sin ^{2}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right) ; {/tex} {tex}x \neq 2 n \pi, n \in {~N}{/tex}
{tex}f(x)=(4 a-3)\left(x+\log _{e} 5\right){/tex} {tex}+2(a-7)\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}\right)\left(\sin \frac{x}{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}f(x)=(4 a-3)\left(x+\log _{e} 5\right)+(a-7)(\sin x){/tex}
Also given that {tex}f(x){/tex} has critical points,
i.e., {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=0{/tex}
{tex}(4 a-3)(1+0)+(a-7)(\cos x)=0{/tex}
{tex}\text { cos } x=\frac{3-4 a}{a-7}{/tex}
As, {tex}\text { cos } x \in[-1,1]{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3-4 a}{a-7} \in[-1,1]{/tex}
{tex}-1 \leq \frac{3-4 a}{a-7} \leq 1{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3-4 a}{a-7} \geq-1{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3-4 a}{a-7} \leq 1{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3-4 a}{a-7}+1 \geq 0{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3-4 a}{a-7}-1 \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3-4 a+a-7}{a-7} \geq 0{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3-4 a-a+7}{a-7} \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\frac{-3 a-4}{a-7} \geq 0{/tex} and {tex}\frac{10-5 a}{a-7} \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\frac{a+\frac{4}{3}}{a-7} \leq 0{/tex} and {tex}\frac{a-2}{a-7} \geq 0{/tex}
{tex}a \in\left[\frac{-4}{3}, 7\right){/tex} and {tex}a \in(-\infty, 2] \cup(7, \infty){/tex}
{tex}a \in\left[\frac{-4}{3}, 2\right]{/tex}

questions.question: Let ${A}=\{1,3,4,6,9\}$ and ${B}=\{2,4,5,8,10\}$. Let R be a relation defined on ${A} \times {B}$ such that ${R}=\left\{\left(\left(a_{1}, b_{1}\right),\left(a_{2}, b_{2}\right)\right): a_{1} \leq b_{2}\right.$ and $\left.b_{1} \leq a_{2}\right\}$. Then, the number of elements in the set R is:
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}{A}=\{1,3,4,6,9\}{/tex} and {tex}{B}=\{2,4,5,8,10\}{/tex}. Let R be a relation defined on {tex}{A} \times {B}{/tex} such that {tex}{R}=\left\{\left(\left(a_{1}, b_{1}\right),\left(a_{2}, b_{2}\right)\right): a_{1} \leq b_{2}\right.{/tex} and {tex}\left.b_{1} \leq a_{2}\right\}{/tex}. Then, the number of elements in the set R is:
questions.explanation:
Given that {tex}{A}=\{1,3,4,6,9\} \& {~B}=\{2,4,5,8,10\}{/tex} and {tex}{R}=\left\{\left(a_{1}, b_{1}\right),\left(a_{2}, b_{2}\right)\right): a_{1} \leq b_{2}{/tex} and {tex}\left.b_{1} \leq a_{2}\right\}{/tex}
Here, if {tex}a_{1}=1,3,4,6,9{/tex}, then {tex}b_{2}{/tex} can take {tex}5,4,4,2,1{/tex} choices, respectively. Also if {tex}b_{1}=2,4,5,8,10{/tex} then {tex}a_{2}{/tex} can take {tex}4,3,2,1{/tex}, choices respectively.
Hence, total number of required relations are
{tex}=(5+4+4+2+1) \times(4+3+2+1){/tex}
{tex}=16 \times 10=160{/tex}

questions.question: If the domain of the function $f(x)=\log _{{e}}\left(4 x^{2}+11 x\right.$ $+6)+\sin ^{-1}(4 x+3)+\cos ^{-1} \frac{10 x+6}{3}(\alpha, \beta]$, then $36|\alpha+\beta|$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: If the domain of the function {tex}f(x)=\log _{{e}}\left(4 x^{2}+11 x\right.{/tex} {tex}+6)+\sin ^{-1}(4 x+3)+\cos ^{-1} \frac{10 x+6}{3}(\alpha, \beta]{/tex}, then {tex}36|\alpha+\beta|{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given that
{tex}f(x)=\log _{{e}}\left(4 x^{2}+11 x+6\right)+\sin ^{-1}(4 x+3)+{/tex} {tex}\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{10 x+6}{3}\right){/tex}
Now, to find domain-
{tex}4 x^{2}+11 x+6 \gt 0,-1 \leq 4 x+3 \leq 1,-1 \leq \frac{10 x+6}{3} \leq 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(4 x+3)(x+2) \gt 0{/tex} and {tex}-4 \leq 4 x \leq-2{/tex} and {tex}-3 \leq 10 x{/tex}{tex}+6 \leq 3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x \lt -2\right.{/tex} or {tex}\left.x \gt \frac{-3}{4}\right){/tex} and {tex}-1 \leq x \leq-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
and {tex}\frac{-9}{10} \leq x \leq-\frac{3}{10}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow x \in\left(-\frac{3}{4}, \frac{-1}{2}\right] \equiv(\alpha, \beta]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \alpha=\frac{-3}{9} \& \beta=\frac{-1}{2}{/tex}
Hence, {tex}36|\alpha+\beta|=36\left|\frac{-3}{4}-\frac{1}{2}\right|=36 \times \frac{5}{4}=45{/tex}

questions.question: For $x \in {R}$, two real valued functions $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ are such that, $g(x)=\sqrt{x}+1$ and $\operatorname{fog}(x)=x+3$ $-\sqrt{x}$. Then, $f(0)$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: For {tex}x \in {R}{/tex}, two real valued functions {tex}f(x){/tex} and {tex}g(x){/tex} are such that, {tex}g(x)=\sqrt{x}+1{/tex} and {tex}\operatorname{fog}(x)=x+3{/tex} {tex}-\sqrt{x}{/tex}. Then, {tex}f(0){/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
Given that {tex}g(x)=\sqrt{x}+1 \& f o g(x)=x+3-\sqrt{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow f(g(x))=x+3-\sqrt{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow f(g(x))=(\sqrt{x})^{2}+3-\sqrt{x}{/tex}
{tex}f\left(g(x)=(g(x)-1)^{2}+3-(g(x)-1)\right.{/tex}
{tex}f(x)=(x-1)^{2}+3-(x-1){/tex}
and {tex}f(0)=(0-1)^{2}+3-(0-1){/tex}
{tex}=1+3+1=5{/tex}

questions.question: The area bounded by the curves $y=|x-1|+\mid x$ $-2 \mid$ and $y=3$ is equal to:
questions.question_raw: The area bounded by the curves {tex}y=|x-1|+\mid x{/tex} {tex}-2 \mid{/tex} and {tex}y=3{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
1. {tex}-\infty \lt x \lt 1{/tex}
    {tex}y=-x+1-x+2=-2 x+3{/tex}
  2. {tex}1 \leq x \lt 2{/tex}
    {tex}y(x-1)-(x-2)=x-1-x+2=1{/tex}
  3. {tex}2 \leq x{/tex}
    {tex}y=x-1+x-2=2 x-3{/tex}
    {tex}y=|x-1|+|x-2|=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}-2 x+3, & -\infty \lt x \lt 1 \\ 1 & 1 \leq x \lt 2 \\ 2 x-3, & 2 \leq x \lt \infty\end{array}\right.{/tex}
    and {tex}y=3{/tex}
    Draw the graph
    Area {tex}=\frac{1}{2}[1+3] \times 2=4{/tex}

questions.question: Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be a function defined by $f(x)=\log _{\sqrt{m}}\{\sqrt{2}(\sin x-\cos x)+m-2\}$ for some $m$, such that the range of $f$ is $[0,2]$. Then the value of $m$ is
questions.question_raw: Let {tex}f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}{/tex} be a function defined by {tex}f(x)=\log _{\sqrt{m}}\{\sqrt{2}(\sin x-\cos x)+m-2\}{/tex} for some {tex}m{/tex}, such that the range of {tex}f{/tex} is {tex}[0,2]{/tex}. Then the value of {tex}m{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}f(x)=\log _{\sqrt{m}}(\sqrt{2}(\sin x-\cos x)+m-2){/tex}
As we know, the range of {tex}a \sin x+b \cos x{/tex} is
{tex}\left[-\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}, \sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Range of {tex}\sin x-\cos x=[-\sqrt{2}, \sqrt{2}]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-2 \leq \sqrt{2}(\sin x-\cos x) \leq 2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-2+m-2 \leq \sqrt{2}(\sin x-\cos x){/tex} {tex}+m-2 \leq 2+m-2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow m-4 \leq \sqrt{2}(\sin x-\cos x)+m-2 \leq m{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \log _{\sqrt{m}}(m-4) \leq \log _{\sqrt{m}}[\sqrt{2}(\sin x-\cos x)+m-2]{/tex}{tex}\leq \log _{\sqrt{m}} m{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \log _{\sqrt{m}}(m-4)=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow m-4=1{/tex} {tex}\left\{\because \log _{a} 1=0\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow m=5{/tex}

questions.question: The number of integral solutions $x$ of $\log _{\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \geq 0$ is:
questions.question_raw: The number of integral solutions {tex}x{/tex} of {tex}\log _{\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \geq 0{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Given that {tex}\log _{\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \geq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x+\frac{7}{2} \gt 0, x+\frac{7}{2} \neq 1{/tex} and {tex}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \gt 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \gt -\frac{7}{2}, x \neq \frac{-5}{2}{/tex} and {tex}x \neq 7{/tex} and {tex}x \neq \frac{3}{2}{/tex}
Now, from eqn. (i)
{tex}\log _{\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \geq 0{/tex}
Case I: If {tex}0 \lt x+\frac{7}{2} \lt 1{/tex} or {tex}\frac{-7}{2} \lt x \lt \frac{-5}{2}{/tex}
then {tex}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \leq\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)^{0} \Rightarrow\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2}-1 \leq 0{/tex}
or {tex}\left(\frac{x-7+2 x-3}{2 x-3}\right)\left(\frac{x-7-2 x+3}{2 x-3}\right) \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{3 x-10}{2 x-3}\right)\left(\frac{-x-4}{2 x-3}\right) \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \leq-4{/tex} or {tex}x \geq \frac{10}{3}{/tex}

No common solution.
Case II:
If {tex}x+\frac{7}{2} \gt 1{/tex} or {tex}x \gt \frac{-5}{2}{/tex}
then {tex}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \geq\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)^{0} \Rightarrow\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^{2} \geq 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(x-7)^{2}-(2 x-3)^{2} \geq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(x-7-2 x+3)(x-7+2 x-3) \geq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(-x-4)(3 x-10) \geq 0(x+4)(3 x-10) \leq 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-4 \leq x \leq \frac{10}{3}{/tex}

So {tex}x \in\left(\frac{-5}{2}, \frac{10}{3}\right]-\left\{\frac{3}{2}\right\}{/tex}
